qa_pairs.question: What are the default memory allocations for APDL jobs in Ansys?
qa_pairs.answer: Ansys allocates 1024 MB total memory and 1024 MB database memory by default for APDL jobs.

qa_pairs.question: How can I manually adjust the default memory allocations for APDL jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: You can manually specify or change these values by adding arguments `-m 1024` and/or `-db 1024` to the `mapdl` command line in your scripts.

qa_pairs.question: What additional arguments might be necessary for APDL jobs when using a remote institutional license server with multiple Ansys licenses?
qa_pairs.answer: When using a remote institutional license server with multiple Ansys licenses, it may be necessary to add `-p aa_r` or `-ppf anshpc` to your `mapdl` command, depending on the Ansys module you are using.

qa_pairs.question: Which type of scripts are generally recommended for Ansys Mechanical (APDL) jobs for better performance?
qa_pairs.answer: The `single node` (SMP Shared Memory Parallel) scripts will typically perform better than the `multinode` (DIS Distributed Memory Parallel) scripts and should be used whenever possible.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important for the Ansys module version to match the input file's generation version for APDL jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: To help avoid compatibility issues, the Ansys module loaded in your script should ideally match the version used to generate the input file.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check which Workbench version was used to create an APDL input file?
qa_pairs.answer: You can check the Workbench version by running `cat YOURAPDLFILE.inp | grep version` on the input file, which will show a line like `! ANSYS input file written by Workbench version 2019 R3`.

qa_pairs.question: In what modes can Ansys Rocky simulations be run?
qa_pairs.answer: Ansys Rocky can run simulations in either graphical user interface (GUI) mode or non-GUI mode.

qa_pairs.question: Does Ansys Rocky support GPU acceleration?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Ansys Rocky supports running simulations with CPUs only or with both CPUs and GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: Which Ansys Rocky module should no longer be used and why?
qa_pairs.answer: The `ansysrocky/2024R2.0` module should be discontinued as it is no longer supported and will be removed.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended Ansys module for Rocky simulations on Alliance clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: It is recommended to use the `ansys/2025R1` module, and future versions that will be installed, as it includes Rocky and is available on all clusters.

qa_pairs.question: How can users view all available command-line options for Ansys Rocky?
qa_pairs.answer: To get a full listing of command-line options, users can run `Rocky -h` on the command line after loading the appropriate Rocky module.

qa_pairs.question: Is there any specific path configuration required to run the `Rocky` command currently?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is currently necessary to set your path to Rocky using `export PATH=$EBROOTANSYS/v251/rocky:$PATH` for the `Rocky` command to be found, although this will be included in future module versions.

qa_pairs.question: When running Rocky with GPUs for coupled problems, how many CPUs should be requested from Slurm?
qa_pairs.answer: When Rocky is run with GPUs for solving coupled problems, the number of CPUs requested from Slurm (on the same node) should be increased to a maximum until the scalability limit of the coupled application is reached.

qa_pairs.question: How many CPUs should be requested for Rocky when running standalone uncoupled problems with GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: If Rocky is being run with GPUs to solve standalone uncoupled problems, only a minimal number of CPUs, such as 2 or possibly 3, should be requested to allow Rocky to run optimally.

qa_pairs.question: What type of licenses are required if Ansys Rocky is run with four or more CPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: When Rocky is run with four or more CPUs (>= 4 cpus), `rocky_hpc` licenses will be required, which are provided by the SHARCNET license.

qa_pairs.question: Are the provided Slurm scripts for Rocky currently guaranteed to work without adjustments?
qa_pairs.answer: No, the provided Slurm scripts for Rocky have not been tested since the clusters were updated in fall 2025, so some adjustments may be required.
